checklist.note: The design/plan omit the approved v2 target grammar (continuous bar spine, group spacer lines, flattened review, de-jargoned stage labels), so the documented contract does not describe what will be implemented.

The design's Visual grammar section asserts the bar is NOT a permanent rail; the approved v2 direction makes it a continuous rail on every line. Implementing v2 against this doc would violate the recorded design decision.
Scope files already cover renderer, tests, getting-started, and both plan docs; no new files needed for v2.

findings.summary: Visual grammar states the bar is used only inside the active block and explicitly 'not as a permanent rail connecting the whole session.' The approved v2 rework inverts this: every non-corner line anchors to a continuous | spine with a blank | spacer between logical groups. Update the design and default-interaction contract to specify the v2 continuous-rail grammar, the group-spacer rule, the flattened single-level review (drop the 'Files' label; inline file names with a middot separator), and the de-jargoned stage line (no 'preflight:' / 'Detection is read-only') before implementation.

The implementation plan has no task covering the v2 continuous-rail renderer rework or the snapshot/transcript test rewrites it requires. Add a task that specifies the spine invariant (every line begins with a glyph+two-space prefix; no bare lines), the one-blank-| between-groups rule, and updates to the integration transcript and getting-started example.

findings.summary: V2 requires live active questions to use the `◆ … / │ ● / │ ○` spine grammar, but freezes the Questionary backend and InteractiveInitUI while the implementation plan scopes changes to RichGuidedRenderer. The renderer never receives Questionary's live option lines, and the representative transcript uses a fake prompt adapter, so this contract is neither implementable nor tested within scope. Expand scope and tests to the prompt boundary, or explicitly exempt live prompt frames.

The core invariant requires every non-corner line to start with a glyph or spine followed by two spaces, but group separators are specified and tested as the literal one-character line `│`. Define the separator as an explicit exception or consistently emit `│  ` so the acceptance assertion is unambiguous.

findings.summary: The renderer-only scope cannot produce the claimed `◆ … / │ ● / │ ○` live frame. The current Questionary adapter supplies `◆` and a `›` pointer but no `│` option prefix, while select prompts default to no selected/unselected indicators. Because the backend is declared untouched and transient frames are excluded from tests, the active interaction remains off-spine despite the design claiming Questionary already draws this shape. Scope and test the prompt adapter, or remove the live-frame/continuous-session claim and explicitly accept the visual discontinuity.

Task V2.2 says the central helper emits only glyph-plus-two-spaces or spine-plus-two-spaces and that no call site may print a bare line, but the authoritative invariant requires bare `│` separator lines with no trailing whitespace. Following this task literally cannot satisfy V2.1. Define the separator as a third helper output and prohibit only bare content lines.

The plan now distinguishes completed v1 work from active v2 work.
The design grammar now explicitly defines bare `│` as the separator exception.

The live-frame claim must describe Questionary's actual native style (qmark + pointer, no | on option lines) and accept the transient off-spine discontinuity; the persistent collapsed answer is what stays on-spine. Renderer-only scope is fine once the design stops claiming the renderer/backend produces an on-spine live frame.

findings.summary: The new authoritative grammar says its renderer fallback is defined by the existing `_RAIL_GLYPHS`/`_EVENT_GLYPHS` tables and that live glyphs are absent from those tables, but `_RAIL_GLYPHS` currently contains renderer mappings for `◆` and `●` and contains no `■` cancellation mapping. V2.2/V2.3 neither require reconciling these tables nor define the intended replacement mapping, so an implementation can follow the declared source of truth while violating the same grammar and portability contract.

The doc must align to the glyphs the v2 renderer actually emits (corners, spine, completed-diamond, warning, fail, plus the started event glyph in verbose) and drop the invented cancel glyph; cancel/fail render on the closer line as the code already does.
Scope covers renderer, tests, docs, plan artifacts.

findings.summary: Concur CODX-009. State the v2 renderer set as corners + spine + completed-diamond + warning + fail (+ started event glyph, verbose), drop the invented cancel glyph (cancel/fail use the closer line as today), and add a plan step to update _RAIL_GLYPHS/_EVENT_GLYPHS so the renderer emits only that set (workspace becomes a completed-diamond answer, not the bullet stage glyph).

The new authoritative grammar says its renderer fallback is defined by the existing `_RAIL_GLYPHS`/`_EVENT_GLYPHS` tables and that live glyphs are absent from those tables, but `_RAIL_GLYPHS` currently contains renderer mappings for `◆` and `●` and contains no `■` cancellation mapping. findings.summary: The prior major is only partially fixed: `_print(...).rstrip()` removes trailing whitespace, but `close_session` still passes `cont_prefix=None`, producing space-indented persistent lines for narrow terminal results (for example `  Recovery: ...`). The binding v2 design requires every wrapped persistent continuation to start with `│  ` and says no other line shapes exist. The new regression test checks only trailing whitespace and therefore accepts the remaining violation. Use a spine continuation, or explicitly revise the binding invariant and test that documented exception.
